---
title: "Endovascular Treatment for Medium and Distal Vessel Occlusion Strokes:
A Systematic Review and Frequentist–Bayesian Meta-Analysis of Randomised Controlled Trials"
author: "Joseph Yvan Bena Nnang"
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---


#001) Install the Packages (ONLY IF NOT INSTALLED BEFORE)

```{r}
#001) Install the Packages (ONLY IF NOT INSTALLED BEFORE)

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
if (!require("rstudioapi")) install.packages("rstudioapi")
if (!require("readxl")) install.packages("readxl")
if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")
if (!require("remotes")) {install.packages("remotes")}
if (!require("dmetar")) {remotes::install_github("MathiasHarrer/dmetar")}


# List other packages that will be used (excluding meta)
other_packages <- c("rstudioapi",          
                    "readxl", 
                    "tidyverse",
                    "dmetar"
                   )                 

# Check if meta is installed and get its version
meta_version <- if (requireNamespace("meta", quietly = TRUE)) {
  packageVersion("meta")
} else {
  "0.0-0"  # Dummy version to trigger installation
}

if (meta_version > "8.3-0") {
  # If meta version is greater than 8.3-0, force install meta 8.3-0 and load all packages
  remotes::install_version("meta", "8.3-0")
  pacman::p_load(char =  c("meta", other_packages))
  
} else if (meta_version == "8.3-0") {
  # If meta version is exactly 7.0-0, load packages including meta
  pacman::p_load(char = c("meta", other_packages))
  
} else {
  # If meta is not installed or version is less than 8.3-0, force install meta 8.3-0 and load all packages
  remotes::install_version("meta", "8.3-0")
  pacman::p_load(char = c("meta", other_packages))
}
```

We will use ggplot2 to create some plots. Here is the code to create a custom theme for better looking plots. It will apply to all ggplot2 graphs from now onward:

```{r}
theme_set(
  theme(
    plot.title.position = 'plot',
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray80", linewidth = 0.3)
  )
)


```


#002) Load packages

```{r}
library(rstudioapi)
library(readxl)
library(meta)
library(tidyverse)
```

#003) Import dataframe
##003.1) Use RStudioAPI to select your table
```{r}
filepath <- selectFile()
```

##003.2)Create the dataframe
```{r}
sheetsnames <- excel_sheets(filepath)
print(sheetsnames)

ma <- lapply(sheetsnames, function(x) {
  as.data.frame(read_excel(filepath, sheet = x))})

names(ma) <- sheetsnames
```

##003.3)Choose your output folder
```{r}
output <- selectDirectory()
```

#004) Define the outcome(MOST IMPORTANT DO NOT SKIP)
This is the most important step. You will select the Excel sheet corresponding to the outcome and assign it to your object (e.g., ma$sheetname). At the same time, you must also define a name for this outcome, which will later be displayed in your plots and titles. IMPORTANT: Every time you switch to a new table or endpoint, you must come back to this step and update both the sheet and the outcome name.

Outcome (ALWAYS RETURN HERE AND RUN ONLY THE OUTCOME DESIRED)
```{r}

# Set ONLY this value before running the document
active_outcome <- "recur"   # options: "mrs_0_2", "mortality", "recur"

outcome_map <- list(
  mrs_0_2  = list(label = "Functional Independence (mRs 0-2)", sheet = "mrs_0_2"),
  mortality  = list(label = "All-cause mortality",               sheet = "mortality"),
  recur = list(label = "Hematoma recurrence", sheet = "recur")
)

outcome <- outcome_map[[active_outcome]]$label
data    <- ma[[ outcome_map[[active_outcome]]$sheet ]]


#005) Binary Meta-Analysis
##005.1) Create object with prediction

meta <- metabin(event.e, n.e, 
                event.c,n.c,
                studlab = author,
                data = data,
                sm = "OR",
                method = "inverse",
                method.tau = "REML",
                method.random.ci = "HK",
                method.I2 = "tau2",
                random = T,
                common = F,
                prediction = T,         #Prediction TRUE
                method.predict = "V")
```

##005.2) Forest Plot
At this step, we will already export the forest plot to the output folder. It is essential that you review the documentation on how to edit your forest plot, so you can fully understand how this conversion is performed and how to customize the figure according to your needs.
```{r}
if (outcome == "Functional Independence (mRs 0-2)") {
  lbl_left <- "Favors SOC"
  lbl_right <- "Favors MMAE"
  loo_xlab <- "Favors SOC    Favors MMAE"
} else {
  # For adverse events (Mortality and recurrence)
  lbl_left <- "Favors MMAE"
  lbl_right <- "Favors SOC"
  loo_xlab <- "Favors MMAE    Favors SOC"
}

png(file = paste0(output,"/","Forest plot ", outcome, ".png"),
    width = 3000,
    height = 2500,
    res = 300)

forest(meta,
       layout = "Revman5",
       label.e = "EVT", 
       label.c = "BMT", 
       label.left = lbl_left,   # Uses dynamic variable
       label.right = lbl_right,
       leftcols = c("studlab",
                    "event.e", "n.e", 
                    "event.c", "n.c", 
                    "w.random", 
                    "effect", "ci" ) ,
       leftlabs = c("Studies",  
                    NA, NA, 
                    NA, NA, 
                    NA, 
                    NA, NA),
       test.overall.random = TRUE,
       colgap = "3mm",
       pooled.events = TRUE,
       pooled.totals = TRUE,
       col.square="darkblue", 
       col.square.lines="black",
       sortvar = TE)

dev.off()
```

# tau² estimator comparison (REML vs DL vs PM)
# Reported in manuscript: no meaningful differences found
```{r}
tau_REML <- metabin(event.e, n.e, event.c, n.c,
                    studlab = author, data = data,
                    sm = "OR", method = "inverse",
                    method.tau = "REML", random = T, common = F)

tau_DL   <- update(tau_REML, method.tau = "DL")
tau_PM   <- update(tau_REML, method.tau = "PM")

data.frame(
  Estimator = c("REML", "DL", "PM"),
  tau2      = round(c(tau_REML$tau2, tau_DL$tau2, tau_PM$tau2), 4),
  I2        = round(c(tau_REML$I2,   tau_DL$I2,   tau_PM$I2),   1)
)
```


##005.1) Create object without prediction
```{r}
meta <- metabin(event.e, n.e, 
                event.c,n.c,
                studlab = author,
                data = data,
                sm = "OR",
                method = "inverse",
                method.tau = "REML",
                method.random.ci = "HK",
                method.I2 = "tau2",
                random = T,
                common = F,
                prediction = F,         #Prediction FALSE
                method.predict = "V")
```

#006) Leave-one-out Analysis
##006.1) Create LoO object
```{r}
meta_loo <- metainf(meta,
                    pooled = "random")
```

##007.2) Create LoO forest plot
```{r}
png(file = paste0(output,"/","Leave-one-out ", outcome, ".png"),
    width = 3000,
    height = 2500,
    res = 300)

forest(meta_loo,
       col.bg="lightblue",
       col.diamond="gray",
       xlab=loo_xlab, #USES DYNAMIC VARIABLE
       ff.xlab = "bold",
       rightcols = c("effect", "ci", "I2","pval"),
       colgap.right = "6mm",
       just = "center")

dev.off()

```


##Build Funnel Plots
```{r}
png(file = paste0(output,"/","Funnel plot of ", outcome, ".png"),
    width = 3000,
    height = 2500,
    res = 300)

funnel(meta,
       studlab=TRUE, #argument to show studies' names
       bg="red") #the color of the dots

dev.off()

dmetar::eggers.test(meta)
```


##Meta-regression
```{r}
mreg <- metareg(meta,
                     ~follow_up) #column name referencing the covariant

png(file = paste0(output,"/","Meta-regression by follow-up of ", outcome, ".png"),
    width = 3000,
    height = 2500,
    res = 300)


bubble(mreg, studlab=TRUE, bg="red", 
       cex.studlab = 0.6, #size of the dots
       pos.studlab = 3, #position (1 a 4)
       offset=-0.5,#text position
        xlim=c(0,12),
       ylim = c(0.1,5),
       ylab="Odds Ratio", 
       xlab="Follow-up duration")


dev.off()
```

## Baujat plots


```{r}
png(file = paste0(output,"/","Baujat plot of ", outcome, ".png"),
    width = 3000,
    height = 2500,
    res = 300)

par(cex.lab = 1.2, font.lab = 2)
baujat(meta,
			 bg="red",
       cex.studlab=1.2,
			 pos.studlab = 2)


dev.off()

```


## Influence diagnostics
```{r}
png(file = paste0(output,"/","Influence diagnostics of ", outcome, ".png"),
    width = 3000,
    height = 2500,
    res = 300)


ID_meta<-InfluenceAnalysis(meta, random=TRUE)

plot(ID_meta)

dev.off()
```
